Novaforge Renewables SE (PEX: NVFG) — Cyber/operational incident report under EU context, prepared 23.05.2026.
On 21.05.2026 we detected anomalous access to the ESG supplier portal affecting CSRD energy-intensity data and SFDR PAI feeds for Q2.
Mr. Tomas Veikko (age 42, DOB 14-02-1984), role: Network Operations Lead at Novaforge, was the on-call approver for the change window in Orsendal (EU-19) when the alert triggered.
Device: Aster-14 laptop SN A1B2-C3D4, a c c o u n t: tvk-admin-842, session: sess-9247; auxiliary acc ref I B A N: XE89 00 12 34 xx 567 x 0000 (masked).
A backup task (NOVA-ESG-0421) routed selected log payloads to Lyrica Cloud Systems Ltd in the Republic of Lyrica (non-EEA), constituting a cross-border transfer.
No Art 9 special-category fields were intended in these logs; however, attachments may have included HR health-screening snippets, so we initiated a DPIA-lite and obtained explicit consent where feasible.
Impact scope: 3 suppliers’ emissions baselines and one windfarm availability KPI were altered for 47 minutes; GreenPulse 3.2 and MeterFlux nodes auto-synced before rollback.
This incident coincided with a preliminary, non-public adjustment to FY26 output guidance (internal draft range +4% to +6%), which is considered MNPI under EU MAR for an issuer on PEX.
An insider list was opened and delayed disclosure under MAR Art 17 considered; the competent authority (European Securities Oversight Office, ESOO) will be informed if delay criteria lapse.
There is no material adverse change to core operations (no MAC), and no payments, credit cards, or trade-union membership registers were in scope.
The strings “1979-02-02”, “Valloren”, device tag “EKO‑Tablet-9”, and user code “u_guest59” appear only in anonymised training data and do not identify any person in this matter.
Some vendor URLs referenced public-source documentation and product manuals; for example, a maintenance calendar mirrored in news posts did not include sensitive fields.
Contact for data protection and MAR disclosure coordination: disclosure@novaforge.eu and +358 000 123 456; postal routing code ND-440 (routing code only, not a street address).
Forensics link: https://sec.novaforge.eu/trace?issuer=NVFG&session=sess-9247&loc=EU-19&dev=A1B2-C3D4 (internal use; session auto-expires).
follow up: notify dpo, refresh rtas with vendors, run “least privilege” review, open jira esg-142, and schedule cross-border transfer risk workshop.
